# Municipal Alpha

> Structured, investable data from US municipal public records. A knowledge graph of 19,500 municipalities built from daily automated collection of agendas, minutes, check registers, permits, RFPs, capital plans, and assessor records.

Municipal Alpha monitors 2,100+ municipalities across 50 states, collecting 300,000+ documents from municipal CMS platforms. Documents are classified by type, entities are resolved to public company tickers, and signals are structured for investment research, infrastructure prospecting, and government intelligence.

## What Municipal Alpha Does

- Reads municipal government portals daily across 15+ CMS platforms (CivicPlus, Granicus, BoardDocs, WordPress, Catalis, Revize, and others)
- Classifies documents into 14 canonical types: agenda, minutes, check_register, budget, capital_plan, ordinance, permit, rfp, bid_tabulation, legislative_document, engineering_report, and others
- Resolves vendor names to public company tickers (100+ tickers across 2,100+ municipalities)
- Cross-references municipal records against federal databases (FCC, FAA, SAM.gov, FERC, EPA, EMMA)
- Tracks contagion patterns as policy spreads across jurisdictions (PFAS remediation, housing density, lead pipe replacement)

## Data Products

- Tower & Infrastructure Leads: https://municipalalpha.com/solutions/tower-leads/
- Municipal Alternative Data: https://municipalalpha.com/solutions/alt-data/
- Municipal Credit Intelligence: https://municipalalpha.com/solutions/credit-intelligence/
- Government Contractor Signals: https://municipalalpha.com/solutions/contractor-signals/
- Infrastructure Risk Indicators: https://municipalalpha.com/solutions/infrastructure-risk/

## Coverage

- 2,100+ municipalities monitored daily
- 50 states
- 300,000+ documents ingested
- 53,000+ classified signals
- 3,800+ tower sites screened with parcel cross-reference
- 43,000+ building permits structured
- 100+ public company tickers resolved from vendor payment data

- Infrastructure risk sample (CSV): https://municipalalpha.com/sample-data/infrastructure-risk-sample.csv

## Why This Data Is Unique

Roughly 99% of the documents in our pipeline are invisible to search engines and AI agents. Municipal CMS platforms store documents at URLs with no navigation link. A town clerk uploads a check register, it gets a URL, and nothing on the site points to it. Google cannot index what it cannot find. We built connectors for each CMS platform that reach into the document store directly.
